title: salix planifolia var. yukonensis
intro: Salix planifolia var. yukonensis, also known as Yukon willow, is a variety of the white willow (Salix planifolia) tree that is native to the boreal forests of North America. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and analgesic compounds such as salicin, a precursor to aspirin. The health benefits of Yukon willow include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as potentially lowering blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Yukon willow can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and are often used to treat conditions such as arthritis and menstrual cramps.
